regions: Europe, Middle East, Africa and India (EMEAI)

key_suppliers: Regional, international and national oil and gas companies
supply_chain.dependencies: Hydrocarbon-based raw materials (ethane, propane, butane, naphtha, condensate) derived from natural gas liquids (NGLs) and crude oil production. Primary feedstocks for ethylene and propylene production. Reliance on natural gas for electricity generation.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions from industry capacity constraints, material availability, global logistics delays, third-party service and material providers. Transportation capacity constraints (ocean shipping containers). Global labor availability constraints. Disruptions from tenant and neighboring manufacturing operations. Geopolitical tensions (Russia-Ukraine conflict, Middle East conflicts) causing supply chain and logistics disruptions, volatility in feedstock/energy prices (especially in Europe). Severe weather events and natural disasters disrupting operations. Cyberattacks and cybersecurity incidents. Concentration risk in certain geographic regions for manufacturing.

regulatory_risks: Extensive federal, state, local and foreign environmental laws and regulations (pollution, climate change, greenhouse gas emissions, waste management)
Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) compliance
Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act (CERCLA/Superfund) liabilities
Hazardous waste operating licenses and remediation obligations
Chemical facility security regulations (U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Coast Guard, TSA Rail Transportation Security Rule)
Evolving regulatory requirements on environmental, health and safety matters
New or more restrictive regulations on plastic waste, extended producer responsibility fees, recycled content mandates, taxes on plastics, bans on non-essential items
Intergovernmental negotiations on international legally binding instrument to end plastic pollution
Carbon pricing and greenhouse gas emissions regulations
Changes in tax laws and regulations across multiple jurisdictions
Trade agreements, import/export controls, taxes, duties and tariffs
Sanctions and export restrictions (e.g., Russia sanctions)
FDA approval requirements (for certain products)
Data privacy regulations (e.g., EU regulations)
Compliance with Responsible Care® and ISO-14001 standards
Nuclear Regulatory Commission permits (for advanced small modular reactor projects)
SEC climate-related disclosure rules (Enhancement and Standardization of Climate-Related Disclosures)
Global Anti-Base Erosion Proposal (GloBE) / OECD Pillar Two framework on taxation
Asbestos litigation and related legal compliance
Product safety and chemical use regulations (health and safety of chemicals in commerce)
Pension and postretirement benefit plan regulations

sanction_risks: U.S., Canada, EU and other countries imposing economic sanctions on Russia due to Ukraine invasion
Suspended purchases of feedstocks and energy from Russia
Stopped investments in and flow of materials into Russia
Potential additional sanctions and impacts from ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict
Operations in countries subject to U.S. economic sanctions and export controls (Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria)
Compliance with U.S. State Department sanctions and export controls
Potential sanctions related to Middle East conflicts
Trade restrictions and tariffs impacting global operations
